summary:
Claim: Clinical diagnosis of candidiasis is confirmed by microscopic demonstration of yeast forms (buds) or hyphae.  

Evidence: - Candida spp., especially C. albicans, typically exhibits budding yeasts and forms hyphae under favorable conditions; these are pathognomonic features in tissue samples. - Hyphal presence indicates invasive disease (e.g., deep fungal infections), whereas yeast-only findings suggest superficial or mucosal involvement.  

Answer: D

Evidence: - Candida spp., especially C. albicans, typically exhibits budding yeasts and forms hyphae under favorable conditions; these are pathognomonic features in tissue samples. - Hyphal presence indicates invasive disease (e.g., deep fungal infections), whereas yeast-only findings suggest superficial or mucosal involvement.  

Answer: D. Answer choice: C
